text: "Honestly, the thing I care most about is finding the real story — like, what actually happened for a specific person or family — and letting that carry the emotional weight rather than leaning on guilt or pressure tactics. I want the writing to feel warm and genuine, not like a corporate pitch, but it still needs to be professional enough that donors trust us. And every piece needs a clear ask at the end; I hate when appeals just sort of... trail off without telling people what to do next. Impact numbers matter too, but only when they're grounded in something concrete, not just thrown out there."
